admin: admin_endpoints::AppState,\n46\t    pod_id: String,\n47\t    redis_store: Option<miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore>,\n48\t    query_capture: Arc<QueryCapture>,\n49\t    peer_discovery: Option<Arc<PeerDiscovery>>,\n50\t}\n51\t\n52\timpl UnifiedState {\n53\t    fn new(config: MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n54\t        let metrics = Metrics::new(&config);\n55\t\n56\t        let master_key = std::env::var(\"MIROIR_MASTER_KEY\")\n57\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| config.master_key.clone());\n58\t\n59\t        let admin_key = std::env::var(\"MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY\")\n60\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| config.admin.api_key.clone());\n61\t\n62\t        let jwt_primary = if config.search_ui.enabled {\n63\t            std::env::var(&config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env).ok()\n64\t        } else {\n65\t            None\n66\t        };\n67\t\n68\t        let jwt_previous = std::env::var(&config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_previous_env)\n69\t            .ok()\n70\t            .filter(|v| !v.is_empty());\n71\t\n72\t        let seal_key = SealKey::from_env_or_generate();\n73\t\n74\t        // Set the key-generated gauge before constructing AuthState\n75\t        // so the metric is accurate from the first scrape.\n76\t        metrics.admin_session_key_generated().set(if seal_key.is_generated() { 1.0 } else { 0.0 });\n77\t\n78\t        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n79\t        let namespace = std::env::var(\"POD_NAMESPACE\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"default\".to_string());\n80\t\n81\t        // Create peer discovery instance (plan §14.5)\n82\t        // Only enabled when running in Kubernetes (POD_NAME is set to a real pod name)\n83\t        let peer_discovery = if pod_id != \"unknown\" {\n84\t            Some(Arc::new(PeerDiscovery::new(\n85\t                pod_id.clone(),\n86\t                namespace,\n87\t                config.peer_discovery.service_name.clone(),\n88\t            )))\n89\t        } else {\n90\t            None\n91\t        };\n92\t\n93\t        // Create Redis task store if backend is redis (must happen before AppState\n94\t        // so redis_store and pod_id are available to admin endpoints).\n95\t        let redis_store = if config.task_store.backend == \"redis\" && !config.task_store.url.is_empty() {\n96\t            let url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n97\t            Some(\n98\t                tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n99\t                    tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(\n100\t                        miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(&url)\n101\t                    )\n102\t                })\n103\t                .expect(\"Failed to connect to Redis for scoped key rotation\"),\n104\t            )\n105\t        } else {\n106\t            None\n107\t        };\n108\t\n109\t        let auth = AuthState {\n110\t            master_key,\n111\t            admin_key: admin_key.clone(),\n112\t            jwt_primary,\n113\t            jwt_previous,\n114\t            seal_key: seal_key.clone(),\n115\t            revoked_sessions: std::sync::Arc::new(dashmap::DashMap::new()),\n116\t            admin_session_revoked_total: metrics.admin_session_revoked_total(),\n117\t        };\n118\t\n119\t        let admin = admin_endpoints::AppState::with_redis(\n120\t            config.clone(),\n121\t            metrics.clone(),\n122\t            redis_store.clone(),\n123\t            pod_id.clone(),\n124\t            seal_key.clone(),\n125\t        );\n126\t\n127\t        Self {\n128\t            auth,\n129\t            metrics,\n130\t            admin,\n131\t            pod_id,\n132\t            redis_store,\n133\t            query_capture: Arc::new(QueryCapture::new(1000)),\n134\t            peer_discovery,\n135\t        }\n136\t    }\n137\t}\n138\t\n139\t// Implement FromRef so that admin_endpoints::AppState can be extracted from UnifiedState\n140\timpl FromRef<UnifiedState> for admin_endpoints::AppState {\n141\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n142\t        Self {\n143\t            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n144\t            topology: state.admin.topology.clone(),\n145\t            ready: state.admin.ready.clone(),\n146\t            metrics:            local_rate_limiter: admin_endpoints::LocalAdminRateLimiter::new(),\n154\t            local_search_ui_rate_limiter: admin_endpoints::LocalSearchUiRateLimiter::new(),\n155\t            rebalancer: state.admin.rebalancer.clone(),\n156\t            migration_coordinator: state.admin.migration_coordinator.clone(),\n157\t            rebalancer_worker:        alias_registry: state.admin.alias_registry.clone(),\n165\t            leader_election: state.admin.leader_election.clone(),\n166\t            mode_c_worker: extracted from UnifiedState\n173\timpl FromRef<UnifiedState> for TelemetryState {\n174\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n175\t        TelemetryState {\n176\t            metrics: }\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t// Implement FromRef so that routes::multi_search::MultiSearchState can be extracted from UnifiedState\n214\timpl FromRef<UnifiedState> for FromRef<UnifiedState> for routes::canary::CanaryState {\n228\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n229\t        // Canary routes require Redis task store\n230\t        let redis_store = state.redis_store.clone()\n231\t            .expect(\"Canary routes require Redis task store (task_store.backend: redis)\");\n232\t        let store: Arc<dyn miroir_core::task_store::TaskStore> = Arc::from(redis_store);\n233\t        Self {\n234\t            store,\n235\t            capture: state.query_capture.clone(),\n236\t        }\n237\t    }\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\t#[tokio::main]\n241\tasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n242\t    // Load configuration (file → env → CLI overlay)\n243\t    let config = MiroirConfig::load()\n244\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Failed to load config: {}\", e))?;\n245\t\n246\t    // Initialize structured JSON logging (plan §10 format)\n247\t    // Fields on every line: timestamp, level, target, message, pod_id\n248\t    // Per-request fields (request_id) are added by telemetry middleware span.\n249\t    let filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()\n250\t        .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new(\"info\"));\n251\t\n252\t    let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n253\t\n254\t    // Build subscriber - conditionally add OTel layer\n255\t    // Note: We rebuild the layers in each branch because the types differ\n256\t    // OTel layer must be applied to the bare registry first\n257\t    if let Some(otel_layer) = otel::init_otel_layer(&config) {\n258\t        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n259\t            .json()\n260\t            .flatten_event(true)\n261\t            .with_target(true)\n262\t            .with_current_span(true)\n263\t            .with_span_list(false);\n264\t        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n265\t        registry()\n266\t            .with(otel_layer)\n267\t            .with(filter)\n268\t            .with(json_layer)\n269\t            .init();\n270\t    } else {\n271\t        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n272\t            .json()\n273\t            .flatten_event(true)\n274\t            .with_target(true)\n275\t            .with_current_span(true)\n276\t            .with_span_list(false);\n277\t        registry()\n278\t            .with(filter)\n279\t            .with(json_layer)\n280\t            .init();\n281\t    }\n282\t\n283\t    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n284\t    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and never\n285\t    // left — its fields propagate to all child spans and events.\n286\t    let _pod_span = tracing::info_span!(\"runtime\", pod_id = %pod_id).entered();\n287\t\n288\t    info!(\n289\t        shards = config.shards,\n290\t        replication_factor = config.replication_factor,\n291\t        replica_groups = config.replica_groups,\n292\t        \"miroir-proxy starting\"\n293\t    );\n294\t\n295\t    // Validate critical secrets at startup (plan §9: \"orchestrator refuses to\n296\t    // start the search UI without it\").\n297\t    if config.search_ui.enabled {\n298\t        let jwt_env = &config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env;\n299\t        match std::env::var(jwt_env) {\n300\t            Ok(v) if !v.is_empty() => {}\n301\t            _ => {\n302\t                anyhow::bail!(\n303\t                    \"search_ui is enabled but {} is not set — refusing to start. \\\n304\t                     Either set the env var or disable search_ui (search_ui.enabled: false)\",\n305\t                    jwt_env\n306\t                );\n307\t            }\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    // Build unified state\n312\t    let state = UnifiedState::new(config.clone());\n313\t\n314\t    // Start health checker background task\n315\t    let health_checker_state = state.admin.clone();\n316\t    tokio::spawn(async move {\n317\t        run_health_checker(health_checker_state).await;\n318\t    });\n319\t\n320\t    // Start rebalancer worker background task (plan §4)\n321\t    if let Some(ref worker) = state.admin.rebalancer_worker {\n322\t        let worker = worker.clone();\n323\t        let pod_id = state.pod_id.clone();\n324\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n325\t            info!(\n326\t                pod_id = %pod_id,\n327\t                \"rebalancer worker task starting\"\n328\t            );\n329\t            // Load any persisted rebalance jobs from previous runs\n330\t            if let Err(e) = worker.load_persisted_jobs().await {\n331\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to load persisted rebalance jobs\");\n332\t            }\n333\t            worker.run().await;\n334\t            error!(\"rebalancer worker task exited unexpectedly\");\n335\t        });\n336\t    } else {\n337\t        info!(\"rebalancer worker not available (no task store configured)\");\n338\t    }\n339\t\n340\t    // Start scoped key rotation background task (requires Redis)\n341\t    if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n342\t        let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n343\t            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n344\t            redis: redis.clone(),\n345\t            pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n346\t        };\n347\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n348\t            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n349\t        });\n350\t\n351\t        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n352\t        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n353\t        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n354\t        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n355\t        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n356\t        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n357\t        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n358\t        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n359\t        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n360\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n361\t            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n362\t            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n363\t                &redis_url,\n364\t                &key_prefix,\n365\t                move |session_id: String| {\n366\t                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n367\t                    revoked_total.inc();\n368\t                },\n369\t            )\n370\t            .await\n371\t            {\n372\t                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n373\t            }\n374\t        });\n375\t    }\n376\t\n377\t    // Load aliases from task store on startup (plan §13.7)\n378\t    // Aliases must be loaded before any request routing to ensure consistent resolution\n379\t    if let Some(ref task_store) = state.admin.task_store {\n380\t        let alias_registry = state.admin.alias_registry.clone();\n381\t        let store = task_store.clone();\n382\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n383\t            info!(\"loading aliases from task store\");\n384\t            match alias_registry.sync_from_store(&*store).await {\n385\t                Ok(()) => {\n386\t                    let count = alias_registry.list().await.len();\n387\t                    info!(count, \"aliases loaded successfully\");\n388\t                }\n389\t                Err(e) => {\n390\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to load aliases from task store\");\n391\t                }\n392\t            }\n393\t        });\n394\t    } else {\n395\t        info!(\"alias loading skipped (no task store configured)\");\n396\t    }\n397\t\n398\t    // Start drift reconciler background task (plan §13.5)\n399\t    // Uses the drift_reconciler from AppState which is already configured\n400\t    if let Some(ref drift_reconciler) = state.admin.drift_reconciler {\n401\t        let drift_reconciler = drift_reconciler.clone();\n402\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n403\t            info!(\"drift reconciler started\");\n404\t            drift_reconciler.run().await;\n405\t            error!(\"drift reconciler exited unexpectedly\");\n406\t        });\n407\t    } else {\n408\t        info!(\"drift reconciler not available (no task store configured)\");\n409\t    }\n410\t\n411\t    // Start anti-entropy worker background task (plan §13.8)\n412\t    // Uses the anti_entropy_worker from AppState which is already configured\n413\t    if let Some(ref anti_entropy_worker) = state.admin.anti_entropy_worker {\n414\t        let anti_entropy_worker = anti_entropy_worker.clone();\n415\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n416\t            info!(\"anti-entropy worker started\");\n417\t            anti_entropy_worker.run().await;\n418\t            error!(\"anti-entropy worker exited unexpectedly\");\n419\t        });\n420\t    } else {\n421\t        info!(\"anti-entropy worker not available (disabled or no task store configured)\");\n422\t    }\n423\t\n424\t    // Start TTL worker background task (plan §13.14)\n425\t    // Uses the ttl_worker from AppState which is already configured\n426\t    if let Some(ref ttl_worker) = state.admin.ttl_worker {\n427\t        let ttl_worker = ttl_worker.clone();\n428\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n429\t            info!(\"TTL worker started\");\n430\t            match ttl_worker.run().await {\n431\t                Ok(()) => {\n432\t                    info!(\"TTL worker exited cleanly\");\n433\t                }\n434\t                Err(e) => {\n435\t                    error!(\"TTL worker exited unexpectedly: {}\", e);\n436\t                }\n437\t            }\n438\t        });\n439\t    } else {\n440\t        info!(\"TTL worker not available (disabled or no task store configured)\");\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    // Start Mode C worker background task (plan §14.5 Mode C)\n444\t    // Processes chunked background jobs (dump import, reshard backfill)\n445\t    if let Some(ref mode_c_worker) = state.admin.mode_c_worker {\n446\t        let mode_c_worker = mode_c_worker.clone();\n447\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n448\t            info!(\"Mode C worker started\");\n449\t            match mode_c_worker.run().await {\n450\t                Ok(()) => {\n451\t                    info!(\"Mode C worker exited cleanly\");\n452\t                }\n453\t                Err(e) => {\n454\t                    error!(\"Mode C worker exited unexpectedly: {}\", e);\n455\t                }\n456\t            }\n457\t        });\n458\t    } else {\n459\t        info!(\"Mode C worker not available (no task store configured)\");\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    // Start peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5)\n463\t    // Periodically performs SRV lookups to discover peer pods\n464\t    if let Some(ref peer_discovery) = state.peer_discovery {\n465\t        let peer_discovery = peer_discovery.clone();\n466\t        let metrics = state.metrics.clone();\n467\t        let refresh_interval_s = config.peer_discovery.refresh_interval_s;\n468\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n469\t            let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(refresh_interval_s));\n470\t            info!(\n471\t                interval_s = refresh_interval_s,\n472\t                \"peer discovery refresh loop started\"\n473\t            );\n474\t            loop {\n475\t                interval.tick().await;\n476\t                match peer_discovery.refresh().await {\n477\t                    Ok(peer_set) => {\n478\t                        let count = peer_set.len() as u64;\n479\t                        info!(\n480\t                            peer_count = count,\n481\t                            \"peer discovery refresh completed\"\n482\t                        );\n483\t                        metrics.set_peer_pod_count(count);\n484\t                    }\n485\t                    Err(e) => {\n486\t                        error!(error = %e, \"peer discovery refresh failed\");\n487\t                    }\n488\t                }\n489\t            }\n490\t        });\n491\t    } else {\n492\t        info!(\"peer discovery disabled (not running in Kubernetes)\");\n493\t    }\n494\t\n495\t    // Start task registry TTL pruner background task (plan §4, Phase 3)\n496\t    // Runs on single-pod with advisory lock; Phase 6 §14.5 Mode A replaces with rendezvous\n497\t    if let Some(ref store) = state.admin.task_store {\n498\t        let store = store.clone();\n499\t        let pruner_config = config.task_registry.clone();\n500\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n501\t            // The pruner runs in its own thread via spawn_pruner\n502\t            let _pruner_handle = task_pruner::spawn_pruner(store, pruner_config);\n503\t            // The handle is dropped here only on process exit\n504\t            info!(\"task registry TTL pruner started\");\n505\t            // Keep this task alive forever\n506\t            std::future::pending::<()>().await;\n507\t        });\n508\t    } else {\n509\t        info!(\"task registry TTL pruner not available (no task store)\");\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    // Start canary runner background task (plan §13.18)\n513\t    // Only enabled when canary_runner.enabled = true and Redis is available\n514\t    if config.canary_runner.enabled {\n515\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n516\t            let store: Arc<dyn TaskStore> = Arc::from(redis.clone());\n517\t            let canary_config = config.canary_runner.clone();\n518\t\n519\t            // Clone config values for the search executor\n520\t            let search_config = config.clone();\n521\t            let search_executor: miroir_core::canary::SearchExecutor = Arc::new(\n522\t                move |index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery| -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn std::future::Future<Output = miroir_core::error::Result<SearchResponse>> + Send>> {\n523\t                    let index_uid = index_uid.to_string();\n524\t                    let query = query.clone();\n525\t                    let config = search_config.clone();\n526\t\n527\t                    Box::pin(async move {\n528\t                        // For canary queries, we execute against the first available healthy node\n529\t                        let node_addresses: Vec<_> = config.nodes.iter()\n530\t                            .map(|n| n.address.clone())\n531\t                            .collect();\n532\t\n533\t                        for address in node_addresses {\n534\t                            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n535\t                                .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_millis(config.scatter.node_timeout_ms))\n536\t                                .build()\n537\t                            {\n538\t                                Ok(c) => c,\n539\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n540\t                            };\n541\t\n542\t                            let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n543\t\n544\t                            // Build the search request body\n545\t                            let mut body = match serde_json::to_value(&query) {\n546\t                                Ok(v) => v,\n547\t                                Err(e) => return Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e))),\n548\t                            };\n549\t\n550\t                            // Add limit to avoid large responses for canary queries\n551\t                            if !body.get(\"limit\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).is_some() {\n552\t                                body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(20);\n553\t                            }\n554\t\n555\t                            let response = match client.post(&url)\n556\t                                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", config.node_master_key))\n557\t                                .json(&body)\n558\t                                .send()\n559\t                                .await\n560\t                            {\n561\t                                Ok(r) => r,\n562\t                                Err(_) => continue,\n563\t                            };\n564\t\n565\t                            if response.status().is_success() {\n566\t                                if let Ok(text) = response.text().await {\n567\t                                    if let Ok(search_response) = serde_json::from_str::<SearchResponse>(&text) {\n568\t                                        return Ok(search_response);\n569\t              let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n796\t                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n797\t                }\n798\t            } else {\n799\t             pruned_count = pruned,\n837\t                \"pruned expired sessions\"\n838\t            );\n839\t        }\n840\t    }\n841\t}\n842\t\n843\t/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n844\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n845\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n846\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n847\t    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n848\t    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n849\t\n850\t    // Per-node shard count\n851\t    let mut n.is_healthy())\n864\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n865\t                if healthy {\n866\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n867\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n868\t                }\n869\t            }\n870\t        }\n871\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n872\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n873\t        } else {\n874\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n875\t        }\n876\t    }\n877\t\n878\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n879\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n880\t    } else {\n881\t        1.0\n882\t    };\n883\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n884\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n885\t\n886\t    for (node_id, resource-pressure gauges.\n892\t///\n893\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n894\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n895\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n896\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n897\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n898\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n899\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n900\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n901\t\n902\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n903\t        if max > 0 {\n904\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n905\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n906\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n907\t        }\n908\t    }\n909\t\n910\t     // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n917\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n918\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n919\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n920\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n921\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n922\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n923\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n924\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n925\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n926\t                }\n927\t            }\n928\t        }\n929\t    }\n930\t\n931\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n932\t    // Peer pod count is now set by peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5).\n933\t    // Leader election is not yet implemented (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n934\t    // Owned shards count will be set by Mode A rendezvous (plan §14.5).\n935\t    metrics.set_leader(\"global\", true);\n936\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n937\t}\n938\t\n939\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n940\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n941\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n942\t        .ok()\n943\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n944\t}\n945\t\n946\t/// Graceful shutdown signal Structured logging, request IDs, and Prometheus metrics middleware.\n2\t\n3\tuse std::time::Instant;\n4\t\n5\tuse axum::{\n6\t    extract::{Request, State},\n7\t    http::{HeaderMap, std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\n22\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n23\t\n24\t/// Request ID wrapper type for storing in axum Request extensions.\n25\t///\n26\t/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n27\t/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n28\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n29\tpub struct RequestId(pub String);\n30\t\n31\timpl RequestId {\n32\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n33\t    ///\n34\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n35\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n38\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n39\t        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n40\t        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n41\t        hasher.write(bytes);\n42\t        let hash = hasher.finish();\n43\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n44\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n45\t    }\n46\t\n47\t    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n48\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n49\t        &self.0\n50\t    }\n51\t\n52\t    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n53\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n54\t        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n55\t            Some(Self(s))\n56\t        } else {\n57\t            None\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n63\t///\n64\t/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n65\t/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n66\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n67\tpub struct SessionId(pub String);\n68\t\n69\timpl Default for SessionId {\n70\t    fn default() -> Self {\n71\t        Self(String::new())\n72\t    }\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\timpl SessionId {\n76\t    /// Get the inner session ID string.\n77\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n78\t        &self.0\n79\t    }\n80\t\n81\t    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n82\t    ///\n83\t    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n84\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n85\t        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n86\t            Some(Self(s))\n87\t        } else {\n88\t            None\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\tpub async fn request_id_middleware(\n94\t    mut req: Request,\n95\t    next: Next,\n96\t) -> Response {\n97\t    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n98\t    let request_id = req\n99\t        .headers()\n100\t        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n101\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n102\t        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n103\t        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n104\t\n105\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n106\t    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n107\t\n108\t    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n109\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n110\t        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    // Process the request\n114\t    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n115\t\n116\t    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n117\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n118\t        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t    response\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Session pinning `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n128\tpub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n129\t    mut req: Request,\n130\t    next: Next,\n131\t) -> Response {\n132\t    // Extract session ID from header if present\n133\t    let session_id = req\n134\t        .headers()\n135\t        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n136\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n137\t        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n138\t\n139\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n140\t    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n141\t        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    next.run(req).await\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t\n148\t/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n149\t#[derive(Clone)]\n150\tpub struct TelemetryState {\n151\t    pub metrics: Metrics,\n152\t    pub pod_id: String,\n153\t}\n154\t\n155\timpl TelemetryState {\n156\t    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n157\t        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n158\t        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n159\t    }\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\n163\tpub struct Metrics {\n164\t    registry: Registry,\n165\t\n166\t    // ── Request metrics ──\n167\t    request_duration: HistogramVec,\n168\t    requests_total: rebalance_documents_migrated: Counter,\n194\t    rebalance_duration: Histogram,\n195\t\n196\t    // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n197\t    multisearch_queries_per_batch: rollover_documents_expired_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n234\t    rollover_last_action_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n235\t\n236\t    // ── §13.18 Canary metrics (feature-gated) ──\n237\t Option<CounterVec>,\n245\t\n246\t    // ── §13.20 Explain metrics (feature-gated) ──\n247\t    explain_requests_total: Option<Counter>,\n248\t    explain_warnings_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n249\t    explain_execute_total: Option<Counter>,\n250\t\n251\t    // ── §13.21 Search UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n252\t    search_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n253\t    search_ui_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n254\t    search_ui_zero_hits_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n255\t    search_ui_click_through_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n256\t    search_ui_p95_ms: Option<GaugeVec>,\n257\t\n258\t    // ── §14.9 Resource-pressure metrics (always present) ──\n259\t    memory_pressure: Gauge,\n260\t    cpu_throttled_seconds_total: Counter,\n261\t    request_queue_depth: Gauge,\n262\t    background_queue_depth: GaugeVec,\n263\t    peer_pod_count: Gauge,\n264\t    leader: GaugeVec,\n265\t    owned_shards_count: Gauge,\n266\t\n267\t    // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n268\t    admin_session_key_generated: Gauge,\n269\t    admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n270\t\n271\t    // ── §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast metrics (always present) ──\n272\t    settings_broadcast_phase: GaugeVec,\n273\t    settings_hash_mismatch_total: Counter,\n274\t    settings_drift_repair_total: CounterVec,\n275\t    settings_version: GaugeVec,\n276\t    settings_divergence_alert_total: Counter,\n277\t    frozen_indexes: GaugeVec,\n278\t\n279\t    // ── §13.7 Alias metrics (always present) ──\n280\t    alias_resolutions_total: CounterVec,\n281\t    alias_flips_total: CounterVec,\n282\t\n283\t    // ── §13.6 Session pinning metrics (always present) ──\n284\t    session_active_count: Gauge,\n285\t    session_pin_enforced_total: CounterVec,\n286\t    session_wait_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n287\t    session_wait_timeout_total: CounterVec,\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\timpl Clone for Metrics {\n291\t    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n292\t        Self Self::new(&MiroirConfig::default())\n380\t    }\n381\t}\n382\t\n383\timpl Metrics {\n384\t    pub fn new(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n385\t        let registry = Registry::new();\n386\t\n387\t        // ── Request metrics ──\n388\t        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n389\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n390\t                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n391\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n392\t        )\n393\t        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n394\t\n395\t        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n396\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n397\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n398\t        )\n399\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n400\t\n401\t        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n402\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n403\t        )\n404\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n405\t\n406\t        // ── Node health metrics ──\n407\t        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n408\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n409\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n410\t        )\n411\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n412\t\n413\t        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n414\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n415\t                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n416\t            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n417\t        )\n418\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n419\t\n420\t        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n421\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n422\t            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n423\t        )\n424\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n425\t\n426\t        // ── Shard metrics ──\n427\t        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n428\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n429\t        )\n430\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n431\t\n432\t        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n433\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n434\t        )\n435\t        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n436\t\n437\t        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n438\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_distribution\", \"Number of shards assigned to each node\"),\n439\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n440\t        )\n441\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_distribution gauge\");\n442\t\n443\t        // ── Task metrics ──\n444\t        let task_processing_age = Histogram::with_opts(\n445\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\", \"Time between task creation and processing start\")\n446\t                .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0]),\n447\t        )\n448\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_processing_age histogram\");\n449\t\n450\t        let tasks_total = CounterVec::new(\n451\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_tasks_total\", \"Total number of tasks by status\"),\n452\t            &[\"status\"],\n453\t        )\n454\t        .expect(\"failed to create tasks_total counter\");\n455\t\n456\t        let task_registry_size = Gauge::with_opts(\n457\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_task_registry_size\", \"Current number of tasks in the registry\"),\n458\t        )\n459\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_registry_size gauge\");\n460\t\n461\t        // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n462\t        let scatter_fan_out_size = Histogram::with_opts(\n463\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\", \"Number of nodes in scatter operations\")\n464\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10.0, 20.0, 50.0]),\n465\t        )\n466\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n467\t\n468\t        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n469\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number of scatter responses that were partial (some nodes failed)\"),\n470\t        )\n471\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_partial_responses counter\");\n472\t\n473\t        let scatter_retries = Counter::with_opts(\n474\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_retries_total\", \"Number of scatter retry attempts due to node failures\"),\n475\t        )\n476\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_retries counter\");\n477\t\n478\t        // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n479\t        let rebalance_in_progress = Gauge::with_opts(\n480\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\", \"Whether a rebalance is currently running (1=yes, 0=no)\"),\n481\t        )\n482\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_in_progress gauge\");\n483\t\n484\t        let rebalance_documents_migrated = Counter::with_opts(\n485\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\", \"Total number of documents migrated during rebalance\"),\n486\t        )\n487\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_documents_migrated counter\");\n488\t\n489\t        let rebalance_duration = Histogram::with_opts(\n490\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds\", \"Duration of rebalance operations\")\n491\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0, 300.0, 600.0, 1800.0, 3600.0]),\n492\t        )\n493\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_duration histogram\");\n494\t\n495\t        // Register all metrics\n496\t        macro_rules! reg {\n497\t            ($m:expr) => {\n498\t                registry.register(Box::new($m.clone())).expect(concat!(\"failed to register \", stringify!($m)));\n499\t            };\n500\t        }\n501\t\n502\t        reg!(request_duration);\n503\t        reg!(requests_total);\n504\t        reg!(requests_in_flight);\n505\t        reg!(node_healthy);\n506\t        reg!(node_request_duration);\n507\t        reg!(node_errors);\n508\t        reg!(shard_coverage);\n509\t        reg!(degraded_shards);\n510\t        reg!(shard_distribution);\n511\t        reg!(task_processing_age);\n512\t        reg!(tasks_total);\n513\t        reg!(task_registry_size);\n514\t        reg!(scatter_fan_out_size);\n515\t        reg!(scatter_partial_responses);\n516\t        reg!(scatter_retries);\n517\t        reg!(rebalance_in_progress);\n518\t        reg!(rebalance_documents_migrated);\n519\t        reg!(rebalance_duration);\n520\t\n521\t        // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n522\t        let (\n523\t            multisearch_queries_per_batch,\n524\t            multisearch_batches_total,\n525\t            multisearch_partial_failures_total,\n526\t            multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n527\t        ) = if config.multi_search.enabled {\n528\t            let q = Histogram::with_opts(\n529\t                HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch\", \"Number of queries in each multi-search batch\")\n530\t                    .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0, 25.0, 50.0, 100.0]),\n531\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_queries_per_batch\");\n532\t            let b = Counter::with_opts(\n533\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\", \"Total number of multi-search batches processed\"),\n534\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_batches_total\");\n535\t            let p = Counter::with_opts(\n536\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total\", \"Number of multi-search batches with at least one query failure\"),\n537\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_partial_failures_total\");\n538\t            let t = CounterVec::new(\n539\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total\", \"Session pin overrides triggered by multi-search tenant routing\"),\n540\t                &[\"tenant\"],\n541\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total\");\n542\t            reg!(q); reg!(b); reg!(p); reg!(t);\n543\t            (Some(q), Some(b), Some(p), Some(t))\n544\t        } else {\n545\t            (None, None, None, None)\n546\t        };\n547\t\n548\t        // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics ──\n549\t        let (\n550\t            vector_search_over_fetched_total,\n551\t            vector_merge_strategy,\n552\t            vector_embedder_drift_total,\n553\t        ) = if config.vector_search.enabled {\n554\t            let o = Counter::with_opts(\n555\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_search_over_fetched_total\", \"Number of vector searches that over-fetched candidates\"),\n556\t            ).expect(\"create vector_search_over_fetched_total\");\n557\t            let m = CounterVec::new(\n558\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_merge_strategy\", \"Count of hybrid merge strategy selections\"),\n559\t                &[\"strategy\"],\n560\t            ).expect(\"create vector_merge_strategy\");\n561\t            let d = Counter::with_opts(\n562\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_embedder_drift_total\", \"Number of embedder drift detections\"),\n563\t            ).expect(\"create vector_embedder_drift_total\");\n564\t            reg!(o); reg!(m); reg!(d);\n565\t            (Some(o), Some(m), Some(d))\n566\t        } else {\n567\t            (None, None, None)\n568\t        };\n569\t\n570\t        // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 sinks, rest bucketed) ──\n571\t        let (\n572\t            cdc_events_published_total,\n573\t            cdc_lag_seconds,\n574\t            cdc_buffer_bytes,\n575\t            cdc_dropped_total,\n576\t            cdc_events_suppressed_total,\n577\t        ) = if config.cdc.enabled {\n578\t            let }\n1025\t}\n1026\t\n1027\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n1028\t///\n1029\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n1030\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n1031\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n1032\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n1033\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n1034\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n1035\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n1036\t\n1037\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n1038\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1039\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n1040\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n1041\t\n1042\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n1043\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n1044\t}\n1045\t\n1046\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n1047\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n1048\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n1049\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n1050\t}\n1051\t\n1052\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n1053\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n1054\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n1055\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1056\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n1057\t    }\n1058\t\n1059\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n1060\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n1061\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1062\t        }\n1063\t    }\n1064\t}\n1065\t\n1066\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n1067\t///\n1068\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n1069\t/// is accurately decremented.\n1070\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n1071\t    metrics: Metrics,\n1072\t    request_id: String,\n1073\t}\n1074\t\n1075\timpl InFlightGuard {\n1076\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n1077\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n1078\t        tracing::trace!(\n1079\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1080\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1081\t            \"request started\"\n1082\t        );\n1083\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n1084\t    }\n1085\t}\n1086\t\n1087\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n1088\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n1089\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n1090\t        tracing::trace!(\n1091\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n1092\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1093\t            \"request completed\"\n1094\t        );\n1095\t    }\n1096\t}\n1097\t\n1098\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n1099\t///\n1100\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n1101\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n1102\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n1103\t    request\n1104\t        .extensions()\n1105\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n1106\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n1107\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n1108\t        .to_string()\n1109\t}\n1110\t\n1111\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n1112\t///\n1113\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n1114\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n1115\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n1116\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n1117\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n1118\t    mut req: Request,\n1119\t    next: Next,\n1120\t) -> Response {\n1121\t    let start = Instant::now();\n1122\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n1123\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n1124\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n1125\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n1126\t\n1127\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n1128\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1129\t    let request_id = req\n1130\t        .headers()\n1131\t        .get_request_id()\n1132\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1133\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1134\t\n1135\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1136\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1137\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1138\t    let span = info_span!(\n1139\t        \"request\",\n1140\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1141\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1142\t        method = %method,\n1143\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1144\t    );\n1145\t\n1146\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1147\t\n1148\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1149\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1150\t\n1151\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1152\t\n1153\t    drop(in_flight);\n1154\t\n1155\t    let status = response.status();\n1156\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1157\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1158\t\n1159\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1160\t    metrics\n1161\t        .request_duration\n1162\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1163\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1164\t    metrics\n1165\t        .requests_total\n1166\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1167\t        .inc();\n1168\t\n1169\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1170\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1171\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1172\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1173\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1174\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1175\t        tracing::error!(\n1176\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1177\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1178\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1179\t            message = %message,\n1180\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1181\t            status = status_u16,\n1182\t            method = %method,\n1183\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1184\t        );\n1185\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1186\t        tracing::warn!(\n1187\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1188\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1189\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1190\t            message = %message,\n1191\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1192\t            status = status_u16,\n1193\t            method = %method,\n1194\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1195\t        );\n1196\t    } else {\n1197\t        tracing::info!(\n1198\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1199\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1200\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1201\t            message = %message,\n1202\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1203\t            status = status_u16,\n1204\t            method = %method,\n1205\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1206\t        );\n1207\t    }\n1208\t\n1209\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1210\t    let mut response = response;\n1211\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1212\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1213\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1214\t        }\n1215\t    }\n1216\t\n1217\t    response\n1218\t}\n1219\t\n1220\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1221\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1222\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1223\t}\n1224\t\n1225\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1226\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> String {\n1227\t    match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1228\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1229\t        Err(e) => {\n1230\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1231\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1232\t        }\n1233\t    }\n1234\t}\n1235\t\n1236\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1237\timpl Metrics {\n1238\t    // ── Request metrics ──\n1239\t\n1240\t    pub fn record_request_duration(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n1241\t        self.request_duration.with_label_values(&[method, path_template, &status.to_string()]).observe(duration_secs);\n1242\t    }\n1243\t\n1244\t    pub fn inc_requests_total(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16) {\n1245\t        self.requests_total.with_label_values(&[method, path_template, &status.to_string()]).inc();\n1246\t    }\n1247\t\n1248\t    // ── Scatter-gather ──\n1249\t\n1250\t    pub fn record_scatter_fan_out(&self, size: u64) {\n1251\t        self.scatter_fan_out_size.observe(size as f64);\n1252\t {\n1980\t        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n1981\t        assert!(headers.get_request_id().is_none());\n1982\t\n1983\t        headers.set_request_id(\"test-id-123\");\n1984\t = RequestId::new();\n1994\t        // RequestId should be exactly 8 hex characters\n1995\t        assert_eq!(id.as_str().len(), 8);\n1996\t        assert!(id.as_str().chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n1997\t    }\n1998\t\n1999\t    #[test]\n2000\t    fn test_request_id_parse_valid() {\n2001\t        // Valid 8-char hex string\n2002\t        let valid = \"abcd1234\";\n2003\t        let parsed = RequestId::parse(valid.to_string());\n2004\t        assert!(parsed.is_some());\n2005\t        assert_eq!(parsed.unwrap().as_str(), valid);\n2006\t    }\n2007\t\n2008\t    #[test]\n2009\t    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_wrong_length() {\n2010\t        // Wrong length (too short)\n2011\t        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abc123\".to_string()).is_none());\n2012\t        // Wrong length (too long)\n2013\t        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abcd12345678\".to_string()).is_none());\n2014\t    }\n2015\t\n2016\t    #[test]\n2017\t    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_non_hex() {\n2018\t        // Contains non-hex characters\n2019\t    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n66\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n67\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n68\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n69\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n70\t\n71\t        for resp in responses {\n72\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n73\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n74\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n75\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += 0.0\n83\t        };\n84\t\n85\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n86\t        let terms = term_df\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n89\t                let idf = if {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_ddbe5789169948f196a177d4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\n4\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n5\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n6\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n7\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n8\t\n9\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n10\t///\n11\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n12\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n13\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n14\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n15\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n16\t    h.finish()\n17\t}\n18\t\n19\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n20\t///\n21\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n24\t/// for deterministic resolution.\n25\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n26\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n27\t        .iter()\n28\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n29\t        .collect();\n30\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n31\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n32\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n33\t    });\n34\t    scored\n35\t        .into_iter()\n36\t        .take(rf)\n37\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n38\t        .collect()\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n42\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n43\t    topology\n44\t        .groups()\n45\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n46\t        .collect()\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n50\t///\n51\t/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_04436bf48b2e485db995524a","session_id":"669d1526-fc18-4021-9142-3e9a509ed3e4","uuid":"2c02b875-01d3-461c-9ad8-8b4cd7aedace","timestamp":"2026-05-23T13:19:49.885Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore miroir-proxy structure"}

     }\n224\t\n225\t        Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no PK constraint found\".to_string()))\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    /// Parse a comma-separated list of string literals.\n229\t    fn the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n142\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n143\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n144\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n145\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n146\t///\n147\t/// Returns read-your-writes.\n153\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n154\tasync fn search_handler(\n155\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n156\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n157\t .to_string();\n179\t\n180\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n181\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n182\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n183\t        Err(e) => {\n184\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n185\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests                        );\n199\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n200\t                    }\n201\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n202\t                }\n203\t                Err(e) => {\n204\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n205\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n206\t                }\n207\t            }\n208\t        }\n209\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n210\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n211\t            &source_ip,\n212\t            limit,\n213\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n214\t        );\n215\t        if !allowed {\n216\t            warn!(\n217\t                source_ip_hash =
