}\n223\t\n224\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n225\t    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n226\t    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n227\t        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n228\t        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n229\t        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n230\t            // It's an alias\n231\t            (index.clone(), targets)\n232\t        } else {\n233\t            // Not an alias - use index directly\n234\t            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n235\t        }\n236\t    } else {\n237\t        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n238\t        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n239\t    };\n240\t\n241\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n242\t    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n243\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n244\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n245\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n246\t            match strategy {\n247\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n248\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n249\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n250\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n251\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n252\t                        sid,\n253\t                        &state.task_registry,\n254\t                        max_wait,\n255\t                    ).await {\n256\t                        Ok(true) => {\n257\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n258\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n259\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n260\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n261\t                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n262\t                        }\n263\t                        Ok(false) => {\n264\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n265\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n266\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n267\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n268\t                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n269\t                        }\n270\t                        Err(_) => {\n271\t                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n272\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n273\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n274\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n275\t                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n276\t                        }\n277\t                    }\n278\t                }\n279\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n280\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n281\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n282\t                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n283\t                }\n284\t            }\n285\t        } else {\n286\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n287\t            (None, None)\n288\t        }\n289\t    } else {\n290\t        // No session header - normal routing\n291\t        (None, None)\n292\t    };\n293\t\n294\t    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n295\t    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n296\t        debug!(\n297\t            session_id = ?sid,\n298\t            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n299\t            strategy = label,\n300\t            \"session pinning applied\"\n301\t        );\n302\t    }\n303\t\n304\t    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n305\t    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n306\t    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n307\t    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n308\t        return search_multi_targets(\n309\t            resolved_targets,\n310\t            body,\n311\t            state,\n312\t            headers,\n313\t            sid,\n314\t            client_requested_score,\n315\t        ).await;\n316\t    }\n317\t\n318\t    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n319\t    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n320\t    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n321\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n322\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n323\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n324\t            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n325\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n326\t                &state.pod_id,\n327\t                &index,\n328\t                sk.generation,\n329\t            );\n330\t\n331\t            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n332\t            sk.primary_key\n333\t        } else {\n334\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n335\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n336\t        }\n337\t    } else {\n338\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n339\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n340\t    };\n341\t\n342\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n343\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n344\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n345\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n346\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n347\t\n348\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n349\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n350\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n351\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n352\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n353\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n354\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n355\t    };\n356\t\n357\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n358\t    let plan = {\n359\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n360\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n361\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n362\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n363\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n364\t            min_settings_version,\n365\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n366\t        ).entered();\n367\t\n368\t        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n369\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n370\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n371\t                &topo,\n372\t                0,\n373\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n374\t                state.config.shards,\n375\t                group,\n376\t            ) {\n377\t                Some(p) => p,\n378\t                None => {\n379\t                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n380\t                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n381\t                    warn!(\n382\t                        pinned_group = group,\n383\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n384\t                    );\n385\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n386\t                }\n387\t            }\n388\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n389\t            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n390\t            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n391\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n392\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n393\t                &topo,\n394\t                0,\n395\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n396\t                state.config.shards,\n397\t                &index,\n398\t                floor,\n399\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n400\t                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n401\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n402\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n403\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n404\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n405\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n406\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n407\t                        })\n408\t                    })\n409\t                },\n410\t            );\n411\t\n412\t            match plan_result {\n413\t                Some(p) => p,\n414\t                None => {\n415\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n416\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n417\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n418\t                        format!(\n419\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n420\t                            floor, index\n421\t                        ),\n422\t                    );\n423\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n424\t                }\n425\t            }\n426\t        } else {\n427\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n428\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n429\t        }\n430\t    };\n431\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n432\t\n433\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n434\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n435\t\n436\t    // Build search request\n437\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n438\t        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n439\t        query: body.q,\n440\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n441\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n442\t        filter: body.filter,\n443\t        facets: body.facets,\n444\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n445\t        body: body.rest,\n446\t        global_idf: None,\n447\t    };\n448\t\n449\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n450\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n451\t        search_key,\n452\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n453\t    ));\n454\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n455\t\n456\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n457\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n458\t\n459\t    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n460\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n461\t        plan,\n462\t        &client,\n463\t        search_req,\n464\t        &topo,\n465\t        policy,\n466\t        &strategy,\n467\t    )\n468\t    .await\n469\t    .map_err(|e| {\n470\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n471\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n472\t    })?;\n473\t\n474\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n475\t    drop(topo);\n476\t\n477\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n478\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n479\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n480\t    }\n481\t\n482\t    // Build response body\n483\t    let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n484\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n485\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n486\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n487\t    });\n488\t\n489\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n490\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n491\t        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    // Build response with optional headers\n495\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n496\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n497\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n498\t\n499\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n500\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n501\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n505\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n506\t    if current_version > 0 {\n507\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n508\t    }\n509\t\n510\t    if result.degraded {\n511\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n512\t    }\n513\t\n514\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n515\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n516\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n517\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n518\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n519\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n520\t            .join(\",\");\n521\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n522\t    } else if result.degraded {\n523\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    let response = response\n527\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n528\t        .unwrap();\n529\t\n530\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n531\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n532\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n533\t    tracing::info!(\n534\t        target: \"miroir.search\",\n535\t        index = %effective_index,\n536\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n537\t        node_count = node_count,\n538\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n539\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n540\t        \"search completed\"\n541\t    );\n542\t\n543\t    Ok(response)\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n547\t///\n548\t/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n549\t/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\n550\tasync fn search_multi_targets(\n551\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n552\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n553\t    state: Arc<AppState>,\n554\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n555\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n556\t    client_requested_score: bool,\n557\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n558\t    let start = Instant::now();\n559\t\n560\t    // Extract session ID if provided\n561\t    let sid = session_id;\n562\t\n563\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n564\t    let source_ip = headers\n565\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n566\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n567\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n568\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n569\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n570\t        .trim()\n571\t        .to_string();\n572\t\n573\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n574\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n575\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n576\t        Err(e) => {\n577\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n578\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n579\t        }\n580\t    };\n581\t\n582\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n583\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n584\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n585\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n586\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n587\t                    if !allowed {\n588\t                        warn!(\n589\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n590\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n591\t                        );\n592\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n593\t                    }\n594\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n595\t                }\n596\t                Err(e) => {\n597\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n598\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n599\t                }\n600\t            }\n601\t        }\n602\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n603\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n604\t            &source_ip,\n605\t            limit,\n606\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n607\t        );\n608\t        if !allowed {\n609\t            warn!(\n610\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n611\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n612\t            );\n613\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n614\t        }\n615\t    }\n616\t\n617\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n618\t    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(ref sid) = sid {\n619\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n620\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n621\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n622\t            match strategy {\n623\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n624\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n625\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n626\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n627\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n628\t                        sid,\n629\t                        &state.task_registry,\n630\t                        max_wait,\n631\t                    ).await {\n632\t                        Ok(true) => {\n633\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n634\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n635\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n636\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n637\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n638\t                        }\n639\t                        Ok(false) => {\n640\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n641\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n642\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n643\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n644\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n645\t                        }\n646\t                        Err(_) => {\n647\t                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n648\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n649\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n650\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n651\t                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n652\t                        }\n653\t                    }\n654\t                }\n655\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n656\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n657\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n658\t                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n659\t                }\n660\t            }\n661\t        } else {\n662\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n663\t            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n664\t        }\n665\t    } else {\n666\t        // No session header - normal routing\n667\t        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n668\t    };\n669\t\n670\t    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n671\t    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n672\t    let primary_target = targets.first().ok_or_else(|| {\n673\t        error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n674\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n675\t    })?;\n676\t\n677\t    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n678\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n679\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n680\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n681\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n682\t                &state.pod_id,\n683\t                primary_target,\n684\t                sk.generation,\n685\t            );\n686\t            sk.primary_key\n687\t        } else {\n688\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n689\t        }\n690\t    } else {\n691\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n692\t    };\n693\t\n694\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header\n695\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n696\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n697\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n698\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n699\t\n700\t    // Use live topology from shared state\n701\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n702\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n703\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n704\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n705\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n706\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n707\t    };\n708\t\n709\t    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n710\t    // should have identical shard layout)\n711\t    let plan = {\n712\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n713\t            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n714\t            primary_target = %primary_target,\n715\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n716\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n717\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n718\t            min_settings_version,\n719\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n720\t            target_count = targets.len(),\n721\t        ).entered();\n722\t\n723\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n724\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n725\t                &topo,\n726\t                0,\n727\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n728\t                state.config.shards,\n729\t                group,\n730\t            ) {\n731\t                Some(p) => p,\n732\t                None => {\n733\t                    warn!(\n734\t                        pinned_group = group,\n735\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n736\t                    );\n737\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n738\t                }\n739\t            }\n740\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n741\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n742\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n743\t                &topo,\n744\t                0,\n745\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n746\t                state.config.shards,\n747\t                primary_target,\n748\t                floor,\n749\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n750\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n751\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n752\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n753\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n754\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n755\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n756\t                        })\n757\t                    })\n758\t                },\n759\t            );\n760\t\n761\t            match plan_result {\n762\t                Some(p) => p,\n763\t                None => {\n764\t                    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n765\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n766\t                        format!(\n767\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n768\t                            floor, primary_target\n769\t                        ),\n770\t                    );\n771\t                    return Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE);\n772\t                }\n773\t            }\n774\t        } else {\n775\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n776\t        }\n777\t    };\n778\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n779\t\n780\t    // Record scatter fan-out size\n781\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n782\t\n783\t    // Build search request for primary target\n784\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n785\t        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n786\t        query: body.q.clone(),\n787\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n788\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n789\t        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n790\t        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n791\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n792\t        body: body.rest.clone(),\n793\t        global_idf: None,\n794\t    };\n795\t\n796\t    // Create node client\n797\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n798\t        search_key,\n799\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n800\t    ));\n801\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n802\t\n803\t    // Use score-based merge strategy\n804\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n805\t\n806\t    // Execute search\n807\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n808\t        plan,\n809\t        &client,\n810\t        search_req,\n811\t        &topo,\n812\t        policy,\n813\t        &strategy,\n814\t    )\n815\t    .await\n816\t    .map_err(|e| {\n817\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n818\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n819\t    })?;\n820\t\n821\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n822\t    drop(topo);\n823\t\n824\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n825\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n826\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n827\t    }\n828\t\n829\t    // Build response body\n830\t    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n831\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n832\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n833\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n834\t    });\n835\t\n836\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n837\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n838\t        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n839\t    }\n840\t\n841\t    // Build response with optional headers\n842\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n843\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n844\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n845\t\n846\t    // Add settings headers\n847\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(primary_target).await {\n848\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n849\t    }\n850\t\n851\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n852\t    if current_version > 0 {\n853\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n854\t    }\n855\t\n856\t    if result.degraded {\n857\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n858\t    }\n859\t\n860\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n861\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n862\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n863\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n864\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n865\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n866\t            .join(\",\");\n867\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n868\t    } else if result.degraded {\n869\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n870\t    }\n871\t\n872\t    let response = response\n873\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n874\t        .unwrap();\n875\t\n876\t    // Structured log entry\n877\t    tracing::info!(\n878\t        target: \"miroir.search_multi_target\",\n879\t        primary_target = %primary_target,\n880\t        target_count = targets.len(),\n881\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n882\t        node_count = node_count,\n883\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n884\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n885\t        \"multi-target search completed\"\n886\t    );\n887\t\n888\t    Ok(response)\n889\t}\n890\t\n891\t/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n892\t/// Strip `_miroir_expires_at` from all hits (plan §13.14).\n893\t/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\n894\tpub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n895\t    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n896\t        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n897\t        obj.remove(\"_miroir_expires_at\");\n898\t        if !client_requested_score {\n899\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n900\t        }\n901\t    }\n902\t}\n903\t\n904\t#[cfg(test)]\n905\tmod tests {\n906\t    use super::*;\n907\t\n908\t    #[test]\n909\t    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n910\t        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n911\t            q: Some(\"sensitive user query about private data\".to_string()),\n912\t            offset: Some(0),\n913\t            limit: Some(20),\n914\t            filter: Some(serde_json::json!({\"email\": \"user@example.com\"})),\n915\t            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".to_string()]),\n916\t            ranking_score: Some(false),\n917\t            rest: wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n27\t/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n28\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n29\tpub struct RequestId(pub String);\n30\t\n31\timpl RequestId {\n32\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n33\t    ///\n34\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n35\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n38\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n39\t        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n40\t        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n41\t        hasher.write(bytes);\n42\t        let hash = hasher.finish();\n43\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n44\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n45\t    }\n46\t\n47\t    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n48\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n49\t        &self.0\n50\t    }\n51\t\n52\t    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n53\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n54\t        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n55\t            Some(Self(s))\n56\t        } else {\n57\t            None\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n63\t///\n64\t/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n65\t/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n66\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n67\tpub struct SessionId(pub String);\n68\t\n69\timpl Default for SessionId {\n70\t    fn default() ->  }\n80\t\n81\t    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n82\t    ///\n83\t    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n84\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n85\t        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n86\t            Some(Self(s))\n87\t        } else {\n88\t            None\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\tpub async fn request_id_middleware(\n94\t    mut req: Request,\n95\t    next: Next,\n96\t) -> Response {\n97\t    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n98\t    let request_id = req\n99\t        .headers()\n100\t        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n101\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n102\t        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n103\t        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n104\t\n105\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n106\t    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n107\t\n108\t    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n109\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n110\t        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    // Process the request\n114\t    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n115\t\n116\t    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n117\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n118\t        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t    response\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Session pinning `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n128\tpub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n129\t    mut req: Request,\n130\t    next: Next,\n131\t) -> Response {\n132\t    // Extract session ID from header if present\n133\t    let session_id = req\n134\t        .headers()\n135\t        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n136\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n137\t        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n138\t\n139\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n140\t    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n141\t        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    next.run(req).await\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t\n148\t/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n149\t#[derive(Clone)]\n150\tpub struct TelemetryState {\n151\t    pub metrics: Metrics,\n152\t    pub pod_id: String,\n153\t}\n154\t\n155\timpl TelemetryState {\n156\t    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n157\t        let pod_id = (feature-gated) ──\n242\t    admin_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n243\t    admin_ui_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n244\t    admin_ui_destructive_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n245\t\n246\t    // ── §13.20 Explain metrics (feature-gated) ──\n247\t    explain_requests_total: Option<Counter>,\n248\t    explain_warnings_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n249\t    explain_execute_total: Option<Counter>,\n250\t\n251\t    // ── §13.21 Search UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n252\t    search_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n253\t    search_ui_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n254\t    search_ui_zero_hits_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n255\t    search_ui_click_through_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n256\t    search_ui_p95_ms: Option<GaugeVec>,\n257\t\n258\t    // ── §14.9 Resource-pressure metrics (always present) ──\n259\t    memory_pressure: Gauge,\n260\t    cpu_throttled_seconds_total: Counter,\n261\t    request_queue_depth: Gauge,\n262\t    background_queue_depth: GaugeVec,\n263\t    peer_pod_count: Gauge,\n264\t    leader: GaugeVec,\n265\t    owned_shards_count: Gauge,\n266\t\n267\t    // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n268\t    admin_session_key_generated: Gauge,\n269\t    admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n270\t\n271\t    // ── §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast metrics (always present) ──\n272\t    settings_broadcast_phase: GaugeVec,\n273\t    settings_hash_mismatch_total: Counter,\n274\t    settings_drift_repair_total: CounterVec,\n275\t    settings_version: GaugeVec,\n276\t    settings_divergence_alert_total: Counter,\n277\t    frozen_indexes: GaugeVec,\n278\t\n279\t    // ── §13.7 Alias metrics (always present) ──\n280\t    alias_resolutions_total: CounterVec,\n281\t    alias_flips_total: CounterVec,\n282\t\n283\t    // ── §13.6 Session pinning metrics (always present) ──\n284\t    session_active_count: Gauge,\n285\t    session_pin_enforced_total: CounterVec,\n286\t    session_wait_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n287\t    session_wait_timeout_total: CounterVec,\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\timpl Clone for Metrics {\n291\t    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n292\t        Self Self::new(&MiroirConfig::default())\n380\t    }\n381\t}\n382\t\n383\timpl Metrics {\n384\t    pub fn new(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n385\t        let registry = Registry::new();\n386\t\n387\t        // ── Request metrics ──\n388\t        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n389\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n390\t                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n391\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n392\t        )\n393\t        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n394\t\n395\t        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n396\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n397\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n398\t        )\n399\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n400\t\n401\t        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n402\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n403\t        )\n404\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n405\t\n406\t        // ── Node health metrics ──\n407\t        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n408\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n409\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n410\t        )\n411\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n412\t\n413\t        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n414\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n415\t                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n416\t            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n417\t        )\n418\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n419\t\n420\t        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n421\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n422\t            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n423\t        )\n424\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n425\t\n426\t        // ── Shard metrics ──\n427\t        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n428\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n429\t        )\n430\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n431\t\n432\t        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n433\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n434\t        )\n435\t        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n436\t\n437\t        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n438\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_distribution\", \"Number of shards assigned to each node\"),\n439\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n440\t        )\n441\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_distribution gauge\");\n442\t\n443\t        // ── Task metrics ──\n444\t        let task_processing_age = Histogram::with_opts(\n445\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\", \"Time between task creation and processing start\")\n446\t                .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0]),\n447\t        )\n448\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_processing_age histogram\");\n449\t\n450\t        let tasks_total = CounterVec::new(\n451\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_tasks_total\", \"Total number of tasks by status\"),\n452\t            &[\"status\"],\n453\t        )\n454\t        .expect(\"failed to create tasks_total counter\");\n455\t\n456\t        let task_registry_size = Gauge::with_opts(\n457\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_task_registry_size\", \"Current number of tasks in the registry\"),\n458\t        )\n459\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_registry_size gauge\");\n460\t\n461\t        // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n462\t        let scatter_fan_out_size = Histogram::with_opts(\n463\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\", \"Number of nodes in scatter operations\")\n464\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10.0, 20.0, 50.0]),\n465\t        )\n466\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n467\t\n468\t        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n469\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number of scatter responses that were partial (some nodes failed)\"),\n470\t        )\n471\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_partial_responses counter\");\n472\t\n473\t        let scatter_retries = Counter::with_opts(\n474\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_retries_total\", \"Number of scatter retry attempts due to node failures\"),\n475\t        )\n476\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_retries counter\");\n477\t\n478\t        // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n479\t        let rebalance_in_progress = Gauge::with_opts(\n480\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\", \"Whether a rebalance is currently running (1=yes, 0=no)\"),\n481\t        )\n482\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_in_progress gauge\");\n483\t\n484\t        let rebalance_documents_migrated = Counter::with_opts(\n485\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\", \"Total number of documents migrated during rebalance\"),\n486\t        )\n487\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_documents_migrated counter\");\n488\t\n489\t        let rebalance_duration = Histogram::with_opts(\n490\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds\", \"Duration of rebalance operations\")\n491\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0, 300.0, 600.0, 1800.0, 3600.0]),\n492\t        )\n493\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_duration histogram\");\n494\t\n495\t        // Register all metrics\n496\t        macro_rules! reg {\n497\t            ($m:expr) => {\n498\t                registry.register(Box::new($m.clone())).expect(concat!(\"failed to register \", stringify!($m)));\n499\t            };\n500\t        }\n501\t\n502\t        reg!(request_duration);\n503\t        reg!(requests_total);\n504\t        reg!(requests_in_flight);\n505\t        reg!(node_healthy);\n506\t        reg!(node_request_duration);\n507\t        reg!(node_errors);\n508\t        reg!(shard_coverage);\n509\t        reg!(degraded_shards);\n510\t        reg!(shard_distribution);\n511\t        reg!(task_processing_age);\n512\t        reg!(tasks_total);\n513\t        reg!(task_registry_size);\n514\t        reg!(scatter_fan_out_size);\n515\t        reg!(scatter_partial_responses);\n516\t        reg!(scatter_retries);\n517\t        reg!(rebalance_in_progress);\n518\t        reg!(rebalance_documents_migrated);\n519\t        reg!(rebalance_duration);\n520\t\n521\t        // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n522\t        let (\n523\t            multisearch_queries_per_batch,\n524\t            multisearch_batches_total,\n525\t            multisearch_partial_failures_total,\n526\t            multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n527\t        ) = if config.multi_search.enabled {\n528\t            let q = Histogram::with_opts(\n529\t                HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch\", \"Number of queries in each multi-search batch\")\n530\t                    .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0, 25.0, 50.0, 100.0]),\n531\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_queries_per_batch\");\n532\t            let b = Counter::with_opts(\n533\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\", \"Total number of multi-search batches processed\"),\n534\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_batches_total\");\n535\t            let p = Counter::with_opts(\n536\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total\", \"Number of multi-search batches with at least one query failure\"),\n537\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_partial_failures_total\");\n538\t            let t = CounterVec::new(\n539\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total\", \"Session pin overrides triggered by multi-search tenant routing\"),\n540\t                &[\"tenant\"],\n541\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total\");\n542\t            reg!(q); reg!(b); reg!(p); reg!(t);\n543\t            (Some(q), Some(b), Some(p), Some(t))\n544\t        } else {\n545\t            (None, None, None, None)\n546\t        };\n547\t\n548\t        // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics ──\n549\t        let (\n550\t            vector_search_over_fetched_total,\n551\t            vector_merge_strategy,\n552\t            vector_embedder_drift_total,\n553\t        ) = if config.vector_search.enabled {\n554\t            let o = Counter::with_opts(\n555\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_search_over_fetched_total\", \"Number of vector searches that over-fetched candidates\"),\n556\t            ).expect(\"create vector_search_over_fetched_total\");\n557\t            let m = CounterVec::new(\n558\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_merge_strategy\", \"Count of hybrid merge strategy selections\"),\n559\t                &[\"strategy\"],\n560\t            ).expect(\"create vector_merge_strategy\");\n561\t            let d = Counter::with_opts(\n562\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_embedder_drift_total\", \"Number of embedder drift detections\"),\n563\t            ).expect(\"create vector_embedder_drift_total\");\n564\t            reg!(o); reg!(m); reg!(d);\n565\t            (Some(o), Some(m), Some(d))\n566\t        } else {\n567\t            (None, None, None)\n568\t        };\n569\t\n570\t        // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 sinks, rest bucketed) ──\n571\t        let (\n572\t            cdc_events_published_total,\n573\t            cdc_lag_seconds,\n574\t            cdc_buffer_bytes,\n575\t            cdc_dropped_total,\n576\t            cdc_events_suppressed_total,\n577\t        ) = if config.cdc.enabled {\n578\t            let e = CounterVec::new(\n579\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_events_published_total\", \"Total CDC events published\"),\n580\t                &[\"sink\", \"index\"],\n581\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_events_published_total\");\n582\t            let l = GaugeVec::new(\n583\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_lag_seconds\", \"CDC delivery lag in seconds\"),\n584\t                &[\"sink\"],\n585\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_lag_seconds\");\n586\t            let b = GaugeVec::new(\n587\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_buffer_bytes\", \"CDC buffer size in bytes\"),\n588\t                &[\"sink\"],\n589\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_buffer_bytes\");\n590\t            let d = CounterVec::new(\n591\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_dropped_total\", \"CDC events dropped due to buffer overflow\"),\n592\t                &[\"sink\"],\n593\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_dropped_total\");\n594\t            let s = CounterVec::new(\n595\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_events_suppressed_total\", \"CDC events suppressed by origin deduplication\"),\n596\t                &[\"origin\"],\n597\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_events_suppressed_total\");\n598\t            reg!(e); reg!(l); reg!(b); reg!(d); reg!(s);\n599\t            (Some(e), Some(l), Some(b), Some(d), Some(s))\n600\t        } else {\n601\t            (None, None, None, None, None)\n602\t        };\n603\t\n604\t        // ── §13.14 TTL metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 indexes, rest bucketed) ──\n605\t        let (\n606\t            ttl_documents_expired_total,\n607\t            ttl_sweep_duration_seconds,\n608\t            ttl_pending_estimate,\n609\t        ) = if config.ttl.enabled {\n610\t            let e = CounterVec::new(\n611\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_ttl_documents_expired_total\", \"Documents expired by TTL sweeper\"),\n612\t                &[\"index\"],\n613\t            ).expect(\"create ttl_documents_expired_total\");\n614\t            let d = HistogramVec::new(\n615\t                HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_ttl_sweep_duration_seconds\", \"Duration of TTL sweep cycles\")\n616\t                    .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n617\t                &[\"index\"],\n618\t            ).expect(\"create ttl_sweep_duration_seconds\");\n619\t            let p = GaugeVec::new(\n620\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_ttl_pending_estimate\", \"Estimated documents pending TTL expiry\"),\n621\t                &[\"index\"],\n622\t            ).expect(\"create ttl_pending_estimate\");\n623\t            reg!(e); reg!(d); reg!(p);\n624\t            (Some(e), Some(d), Some(p))\n625\t        } else {\n626\t            (None, None, None)\n627\t        };\n628\t\n629\t        // ── §13.15 Tenant affinity metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n630\t        let (\n631\t            tenant_queries_total,\n632\t            tenant_pinned_groups,\n633\t            tenant_fallback_total,\n634\t        ) = if config.tenant_affinity.enabled {\n635\t            let q = CounterVec::new(\n636\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_queries_total\", \"Queries routed per tenant and group\"),\n637\t                &[\"tenant\", \"group\"],\n638\t            ).expect(\"create tenant_queries_total\");\n639\t            let p = GaugeVec::new(\n640\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_pinned_groups\", \"Current pinned group per tenant\"),\n641\t                &[\"tenant\"],\n642\t            ).expect(\"create tenant_pinned_groups\");\n643\t            let f = CounterVec::new(\n644\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_fallback_total\", \"Tenant affinity fallback invocations\"),\n645\t                &[\"reason\"],\n646\t            ).expect(\"create tenant_fallback_total\");\n647\t            reg!(q); reg!(p); reg!(f);\n648\t            (Some(q), Some(p), Some(f))\n649\t        } else {\n650\t            (None, None, None)\n651\t        };\n652\t\n653\t        // ── §13.16 Shadow traffic metrics ──\n654\t        let (\n655\t            shadow_diff_total,\n656\t            shadow_kendall_tau,\n657\t            shadow_latency_delta_seconds,\n658\t            shadow_errors_total,\n659\t        ) = if config.shadow.enabled {\n660\t encode_metrics(&self) -> Result<String, prometheus::Error> {\n1009\t        let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n1010\t        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n1011\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n1012\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n1013\t        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n1014\t            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n1015\t        })?)\n1016\t    }\n1017\t\n1018\t    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n1019\t        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n1020\t    }\n1021\t\n1022\t    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n1023\t        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n1024\t    }\n1025\t}\n1026\t\n1027\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n1028\t///\n1029\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n1030\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n1031\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n1032\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n1033\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n1034\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n1035\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n1036\t\n1037\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n1038\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1039\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n1040\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n1041\t\n1042\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n1043\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n1044\t}\n1045\t\n1046\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n1047\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n1048\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n1049\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n1050\t}\n1051\t\n1052\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n1053\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n1054\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n1055\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1056\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n1057\t    }\n1058\t\n1059\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n1060\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n1061\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1062\t        }\n1063\t    }\n1064\t}\n1065\t\n1066\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n1067\t///\n1068\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n1069\t/// is accurately decremented.\n1070\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n1071\t    metrics: Metrics,\n1072\t    request_id: String,\n1073\t}\n1074\t\n1075\timpl InFlightGuard {\n1076\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n1077\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n1078\t        tracing::trace!(\n1079\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1080\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1081\t            \"request started\"\n1082\t        );\n1083\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n1084\t    }\n1085\t}\n1086\t\n1087\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n1088\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n1089\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n1090\t        tracing::trace!(\n1091\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n1092\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1093\t            \"request completed\"\n1094\t        );\n1095\t    }\n1096\t}\n1097\t\n1098\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n1099\t///\n1100\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n1101\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n1102\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n1103\t    request\n1104\t        .extensions()\n1105\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n1106\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n1107\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n1108\t        .to_string()\n1109\t}\n1110\t\n1111\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n1112\t///\n1113\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n1114\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n1115\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n1116\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n1117\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n1118\t    mut req: Request,\n1119\t    next: Next,\n1120\t) -> Response {\n1121\t    let start = Instant::now();\n1122\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n1123\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n1124\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n1125\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n1126\t\n1127\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n1128\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1129\t    let request_id = req\n1130\t        .headers()\n1131\t        .get_request_id()\n1132\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1133\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1134\t\n1135\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1136\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1137\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1138\t    let span = info_span!(\n1139\t        \"request\",\n1140\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1141\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1142\t        method = %method,\n1143\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1144\t    );\n1145\t\n1146\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1147\t\n1148\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1149\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1150\t\n1151\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1152\t\n1153\t    drop(in_flight);\n1154\t\n1155\t    let status = response.status();\n1156\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1157\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1158\t\n1159\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1160\t    metrics\n1161\t        .request_duration\n1162\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1163\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1164\t    metrics\n1165\t        .requests_total\n1166\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1167\t        .inc();\n1168\t\n1169\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1170\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1171\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1172\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1173\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1174\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1175\t        tracing::error!(\n1176\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1177\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1178\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1179\t            message = %message,\n1180\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1181\t            status = status_u16,\n1182\t            method = %method,\n1183\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1184\t        );\n1185\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1186\t        tracing::warn!(\n1187\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1188\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1189\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1190\t            message = %message,\n1191\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1192\t            status = status_u16,\n1193\t            method = %method,\n1194\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1195\t        );\n1196\t    } else {\n1197\t        tracing::info!(\n1198\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1199\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1200\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1201\t            message = %message,\n1202\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1203\t            status = status_u16,\n1204\t            method = %method,\n1205\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1206\t        );\n1207\t    }\n1208\t\n1209\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1210\t    let mut response = response;\n1211\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1212\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1213\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1214\t        }\n1215\t    }\n1216\t\n1217\t    response\n1218\t}\n1219\t\n1220\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1221\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1222\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1223\t}\n1224\t\n1225\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1226\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> String {\n1227\t    match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1228\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1229\t        Err(e) => {\n1230\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1231\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1232\t        }\n1233\t    }\n1234\t}\n1235\t\n1236\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1237\timpl Metrics {\n1238\t    // ── Request metrics ──\n1239\t\n1240\t    pub fn record_request_duration(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n1241\t        self.request_duration.with_label_values(&[method, path_template, &status.to_string()]).observe(duration_secs);\n1242\t    }\n1243\t\n1244\t    pub fn inc_requests_total(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16) {\n1245\t        self.requests_total.with_label_values(&[method, path_template, &status.to_string()]).inc();\n1246\t    }\n1247\t\n1248\t    // ── Scatter-gather ──\n1249\t\n1250\t    pub fn record_scatter_fan_out(&self, size: u64) {\n1251\t        self.scatter_fan_out_size.observe(size as f64);\n1252\t \"abcd1234\";\n2003\t        let parsed = RequestId::parse(valid.to_string());\n2004\t        assert!(parsed.is_some());\n2005\t        assert_eq!(parsed.unwrap().as_str(), valid);\n2006\t    }\n2007\t\n2008\t    #[test]\n2009\t    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_wrong_length() {\n2010\t        // Wrong length (too short)\n2011\t        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abc123\".to_string()).is_none());\n2012\t        // Wrong length (too long)\n2013\t        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abcd12345678\".to_string()).is_none());\n2014\t    }\n2015\t\n2016\t    #[test]\n2017\t    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_non_hex() {\n2018\t        // Contains non-hex characters\n2019\t        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abcd1234!\".to_string()).is_none());\n2020\t        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"ghijklmn\".to_string()).is_none());\n2021\t    }\n2022\t\n2023\t    #[test]\n2024\t    fn test_request_id_uniqueness() {\n2025\t        // Generate two consecutive IDs - they should be different\n2026\t        // due to UUIDv7's timestamp component\n2027\t        let id1 = RequestId::new();\n2028\t        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n2029\t        let id2 = RequestId::new();\n2030\t\n2031\t        assert_ne!(id1, id2);\n2032\t        assert_ne!(id1.as_str(), id2.as_str());\n2033\t    }\n2034\t\n2035\t    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n2036\t    // Integration tests for request_id_middleware\n2037\t    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n2038\t\n2039\t    #[tokio::test]\n2040\t    async fn test_request_id_middleware_adds_header() {\n2041\t        use axum::{routing::get, Router};\n2042\t        use http_body_util::Full;\n2043\t        use tower::ServiceExt;\n2044\t\n2045\t        // Build a simple router with the request ID middleware\n2046\t        let app = Router::new()\n2047\t            .route(\"/test\", get(|| async { \"OK\" }))\n2048\t            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n2049\t\n2050\t        // Create a test request\n2051\t        let request = Request::builder()\n2052\t            .uri(\"/test\")\n2053\t            .body(Full::default())\n2054\t            .unwrap();\n2055\t\n2056\t        // Send the request\n2057\t        let response = app.oneshot(request).await.unwrap();\n2058\t\n2059\t        // Verify X-Request-Id header is present\n2060\t        let header = response\n2061\t            .headers()\n2062\t            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n2063\t            .expect(\"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n2064\t        let header_value = header.to_str().unwrap();\n2065\t\n2066\t        // Verify it's 8 hex characters\n2067\t        assert_eq!(\n2068\t            header_value.len(),\n2069\t            8,\n2070\t            \"X-Request-Id should be 8 characters\"\n2071\t        );\n2072\t        assert!(\n2073\t            header_value.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()),\n2074\t            \"X-Request-Id should be hexadecimal\"\n2075\t        );\n2076\t    }\n2077\t\n2078\t    #[tokio::test]\n2079\t    async fn test_request_id_middleware_unique_per_request() {\n2080\t        use axum::{routing::get, Router};\n2081\t        use http_body_util::Full;\n2082\t        use tower::ServiceExt;\n2083\t\n2084\t        // Build a simple router with the request ID middleware\n2085\t        let app = Router::new()\n2086\t            .route(\"/test\", get(|| async { \"OK\" }))\n2087\t            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n2088\t\n2089\t        // Create two identical requests\n2090\t        let request1 = Request::builder()\n2091\t            .uri(\"/test\")\n2092\t            .body(Full::default())\n2093\t            .unwrap();\n2094\t\n2095\t        let request2 = Request::builder()\n2096\t            .uri(\"/test\")\n2097\t            .body(Full::default())\n2098\t            .unwrap();\n2099\t\n2100\t        // Send both requests\n2101\t        let response1 = app.clone().oneshot(request1).await.unwrap();\n2102\t        let response2 = app.oneshot(request2).await.unwrap();\n2103\t\n2104\t        // Extract headers\n2105\t        let id1 = response1\n2106\t            .headers()\n2107\t            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n2108\t            .unwrap()\n2109\t            .to_str()\n2110\t            .unwrap();\n2111\t        let id2 = response2\n2112\t            .headers()\n2113\t            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n2114\t            .unwrap()\n2115\t            .to_str()\n2116\t            .unwrap();\n2117\t\n2118\t        // Verify IDs are different (UUIDv7 timestamp ensures this)\n2119\t        assert_ne!(\n2120\t            id1, id2,\n2121\t            \"Two consecutive requests should have different request IDs\"\n2122\t        );\n2123\t\n2124\t        // Both should still be valid 8-char hex\n2125\t        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 8);\n2126\t        assert_eq!(id2.len(), 8);\n2127\t    }\n2128\t\n2129\t    #[tokio::test]\n2130\t    async fn test_request_id_middleware_preserves_existing_header() {\n2131\t        use axum::{routing::get, Router};\n2132\t        use http_body_util::Full;\n2133\t        use tower::ServiceExt;\n2134\t\n2135\t        // Build a simple router with the request ID middleware\n2136\t        let app = Router::new()\n2137\t            .route(\"/test\", get(|| async { \"OK\" }))\n2138\t            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n2139\t\n2140\t        // Create a request with a pre-existing X-Request-Id header\n2141\t        let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n2142\t Structured logging, request IDs, and Prometheus metrics middleware.\n\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Request, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n    Router,\n    routing::get,\n};\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse prometheus::{\n    Counter, CounterVec, Encoder, Gauge, GaugeVec, Histogram, HistogramOpts, HistogramVec, Opts,\n    Registry, TextEncoder,\n};\nuse tracing::info_span;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\nuse hex;\nuse std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n\n/// Request ID wrapper type for storing in axum Request extensions.\n///\n/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct RequestId(pub String);\n\nimpl RequestId {\n    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n    ///\n    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        hasher.write(bytes);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct SessionId(pub String);\n\nimpl Default for SessionId {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self(String::new())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SessionId {\n    /// Get the inner session ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n    ///\n    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn request_id_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n\n    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    // Process the request\n    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\npub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Extract session ID from header if present\n    let session_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n    }\n\n    next.run(req).await\n}\n\n\n/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TelemetryState {\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl TelemetryState {\n    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\npub struct Metrics {\n    registry: Registry,\n\n    // ── Request metrics ──\n    request_duration: Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\", \"Total number of multi-search batches processed\"),\n            ).expect(\"create multisearch_batches_total\");\n            let p = Counter::with_opts(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total\", \"Number of multi-search batches with at least one query failure\"),\n            ).expect(\"create multisearch_partial_failures_total\");\n            let t = CounterVec::new(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total\", \"Session pin overrides triggered by multi-search tenant routing\"),\n                &[\"tenant\"],\n            ).expect(\"create multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total\");\n            reg!(q); reg!(b); reg!(p); reg!(t);\n            (Some(q), Some(b), Some(p), Some(t))\n        } else {\n            (None, None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics ──\n        let (\n     (None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 sinks, rest bucketed) ──\n        let (\n            cdc_events_published_total,\n       reg!(alias_resolutions_total);\n        reg!(alias_flips_total);\n\n        // ── §13.6 Session pinning metrics (always present) ──\n        let session_active_count = Gauge::new(\n operations\",\n            )\n            .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0]),\n        ).expect(\"create session_wait_duration_seconds\");\n        let reg!(session_wait_timeout_total);\n\n        Self {\n            registry,\n            request_duration,\n            requests_total,\n            requests_in_flight,\n            node_healthy,\n            node_request_duration,\n            node_errors,\n            shard_coverage,\n            degraded_shards,\n            shard_distribution,\n            task_processing_age,\n         multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n            vector_search_over_fetched_total,\n            vector_merge_strategy,\n            vector_embedder_drift_total,\n
